children.name: Anguilla anguilla
children.description: The European eel is a species of eel. Their life history was a mystery for thousands of years, and mating in the wild has not yet been observed. The five stages of their development were originally thought to be different species.

children.name: Anguilla australis
children.description: The short-finned eel, also known as the shortfin eel, is one of the 15 species of eel in the family Anguillidae. It is native to the lakes, dams and coastal rivers of south-eastern Australia, New Zealand, and much of the South Pacific, including New Caledonia, Norfolk Island, Lord Howe Island, Tahiti, and Fiji.

children.name: Anguilla bengalensis
children.description: The mottled eel, also known as the African mottled eel, the Indian longfin eel, the Indian mottled eel, the long-finned eel or the river eel, is a demersal, catadromous eel in the family Anguillidae. It was described by John McClelland in 1844. It is a tropical, freshwater eel which is known from East Africa, Bangladesh, Andaman Islands, Mozambique, Malawi, Sri Lanka, Sumatra, and Indonesia and recently from Madagascar.

children.name: Anguilla bicolor
children.description: Anguilla bicolor is a species of eel in the genus Anguilla of the family Anguillidae, consisting of two subspecies.

children.name: Anguilla borneensis
children.description: Anguilla borneensis, also known as the Borneo eel is a species of the genus Anguilla found in Borneo.

children.name: Anguilla celebesensis
children.description: The Celebes longfin eel is an eel in the family Anguillidae. It was described by Johann Jakob Kaup in 1856. It is a tropical eel known from freshwaters in the Western Pacific, including Indonesia, the Philippines, New Guinea, and Western and American Samoa.

children.name: Anguilla dieffenbachii
children.description: The New Zealand longfin eel is a species of freshwater eel that is endemic to New Zealand. It is the largest freshwater eel in New Zealand and the only endemic species – the other eels found in New Zealand are the native shortfin eel, also found in Australia, and the naturally introduced Australian longfin eel. Longfin eels are long-lived, migrating to the Pacific Ocean near Tonga to breed at the end of their lives.

children.name: Anguilla interioris
children.description: The Highlands long-finned eel is an eel in the family Anguillidae. It was described by Gilbert Percy Whitley in 1938. It is a tropical eel known from freshwaters in eastern New Guinea.

children.name: Anguilla japonica
children.description: The Japanese eel is a species of anguillid eel found in Japan, Korea, Taiwan, China, and Vietnam, as well as the northern Philippines. Like all the eels of the genus Anguilla and the family Anguillidae, it is catadromous, meaning it spawns in the sea but lives most of its adult life in freshwater.

children.name: Anguilla labiata
children.description: Anguilla labiata is a freshwater eel species native to its native range regions.

children.name: Anguilla luzonensis
children.description: The Philippine mottled eel is an eel in the family Anguillidae. It was described by Shun Watanabe, Jun Aoyama, and Katsumi Tsukamoto in 2009. It is a tropical eel known from the Pinacanauan River system on Luzon Island, in the Philippines.

children.name: Anguilla malgumora
children.description: Anguilla malgumora is a freshwater eel species native to Asia regions.

children.name: Anguilla marmorata
children.description: Anguilla marmorata is a freshwater eel species native to Asia, Africa, Oceania regions.

children.name: Anguilla megastoma
children.description: Anguilla megastoma is a freshwater eel species native to Oceania regions.

children.name: Anguilla mossambica
children.description: Anguilla mossambica is a freshwater eel species native to Africa regions.

children.name: Anguilla nebulosa
children.description: Anguilla nebulosa is a freshwater eel species native to Africa regions.

children.name: Anguilla obscura
children.description: Anguilla obscura is a freshwater eel species native to Oceania regions.

children.name: Anguilla reinhardtii
children.description: Anguilla reinhardtii is a freshwater eel species native to Australia regions.

children.name: Anguilla rostrata
children.description: Anguilla rostrata is a freshwater eel species native to North America regions.
